Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to write a DuckDB SQL query that meets the requirements Given table event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp We need to identify cases that have a sequence of three activities in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in that exact order with no other event from the same case in between them Among those cases only those where the time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event of that sequence is more than 5 days Exclude those cases from the final result return all events from event_log except those that belong to cases matching both the sequence and timing condition

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we need to find cases that have the sequence with the time condition then filter out all events belonging to those cases The final query should return all events from cases that are NOT in the set of excluded cases

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Approach For each case we need to detect if there exists a sequence of three consecutive events by timestamp order with those activities The events must be in direct succession meaning that if we order events within a case by timestamp there should be three consecutive rows with these activities in order So we can use LEAD LAG to look at next activities and timestamps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For each case we can compute for each event next activity next timestamp and the one after that Then check if the events activity is Approve Purchase Requisition next is Create Order and next after that is Confirm Order Also we need to ensure there are no other events between them using LEAD offset ensures that the next event is the immediate successor So LEAD activity 1 and LEAD activity 2 will give the next and next next activities Similarly LEAD timestamp 1 and LEAD timestamp 2 for timestamps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we can filter rows where activity Approve Purchase Requisition LEAD activity 1 Create Order LEAD activity 2 Confirm Order That identifies the start of the sequence For that row we have the timestamp of Create Order as LEAD timestamp 1 and timestamp of Confirm Order as LEAD timestamp 2 Then compute the difference LEAD timestamp 2 minus LEAD timestamp 1 greater than interval 5 day

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we collect the case_ids that satisfy this condition Since a case might have multiple such sequences we just need to know if at least one such sequence exists So we can select distinct case_id from the event_log where the above condition holds Then final query select all from event_log where case_id not in excluded case ids

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to return all events from event_log except those belonging to excluded cases So we filter out those cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Need to ensure that the interval subtraction is correct In DuckDB subtracting timestamps yields an interval Then comparison with interval 5 day is allowed

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Alternatively we could use datediff but careful datediff with day counts day boundaries not exact 24h periods The requirement says time elapsed between is more than 5 days That likely means duration greater than 5 days that is greater than 5 times 24 hours So using interval subtraction and comparing with interval 5 day is precise

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Using LEAD with offset 1 and 2 checks that the next event after Approve Purchase Requisition is Create Order and the one after that is Confirm Order That ensures direct succession because LEAD offset picks the nth next row in the ordered partition So if there is an activity between them the next activity after Approve Purchase Requisition would not be Create Order and next next would not be Confirm Order So that works
